fields.label: Policy Name
fields.description: A unique name for this memory policy. Use descriptive names that indicate the policy's purpose.

fields.label: Enabled
fields.description: Enable or disable this policy. Disabled policies will not capture memories even if assigned to agents.

fields.label: Description
fields.description: Describe when and how this policy should be used. Helps users choose the right policy for their needs.

fields.label: Agent
fields.description: The agent this policy applies to. Leave empty to create a reusable policy template.

fields.label: Memory Profile
fields.description: The memory profile that defines the schema and default behavior for this policy.

fields.label: Capture Owner
fields.description: Who produces the memory: main_agent (the agent itself), memory_agent (dedicated capture agent), post_run_llm (separate LLM call), or rules_only (no LLM, pattern-based).

fields.label: Memory Agent
fields.description: The dedicated agent used for memory capture when Capture Owner is set to 'memory_agent'.

fields.label: Capture Stage
fields.description: When memory capture occurs: in_prompt (during generation), post_response_sync (immediately after), post_response_async (background), conversation_end, or scheduled.

fields.label: Capture Frequency Type
fields.options:
every_run
every_n_runs
every_n_turns
conversation_end
manual
scheduled
fields.description: How often to capture: every_run (each time), every_n_runs (periodic), every_n_turns (after N messages), conversation_end, manual (explicit only), or scheduled.

fields.label: Capture Frequency Value
fields.description: The N value for 'every_n_runs' or 'every_n_turns' frequency types. E.g., 3 means capture every 3rd run or turn.

fields.label: Conversation End Strategy
fields.description: How to determine when a conversation ends: manual_close (user action), idle_timeout (inactivity), heuristic (AI detection), or never (must manually trigger).

fields.label: Idle Timeout (Minutes)
fields.description: Minutes of inactivity before a conversation is considered ended. Memories are captured when timeout occurs.

fields.label: Capture Prompt
fields.description: Custom prompt to guide memory extraction. Overrides the profile's default capture prompt. Be specific about what to capture and how to format it.

fields.label: Capture Schema (JSON)
fields.description: JSON Schema for validating captured memories. If a profile is selected, this inherits from the profile but can be overridden here.

fields.label: Allow Open Schema
fields.description: Allow any JSON structure without schema validation. Useful for flexible capture but may result in inconsistent data.

fields.label: Require JSON Schema Match
fields.description: Require captured memories to strictly match the JSON Schema. Memories that don't match will be rejected.

fields.label: Allow Update Existing
fields.description: Allow updating existing memories when similar content is captured again. Prevents duplicate memories.

fields.label: Allow Merge
fields.description: Merge new data with existing memories rather than replacing them. Useful for accumulating information over time.

fields.label: Allow Append
fields.description: Append new items to lists within existing memories. Useful for collecting multiple related items.

fields.label: Minimum Confidence
fields.description: Minimum confidence score (0-1) for accepting memories. Memories below this threshold are rejected. Higher values = higher quality, fewer memories.

fields.label: Store Raw Payload
fields.description: Store the raw, unprocessed capture output. Useful for debugging but increases storage usage.

fields.label: Store Summary
fields.description: Generate and store a text summary of the memory. Enables text-based search and human review.

fields.label: Enable FTS Index
fields.description: Enable Full-Text Search indexing for keyword-based memory retrieval. Recommended for most use cases.

fields.label: Enable Vector Index
fields.description: Enable vector/semantic indexing for similarity-based memory retrieval. Requires more storage but enables semantic search.

fields.label: Vector Backend
fields.description: Backend for vector storage: sqlite_vec (embedded), pgvector (PostgreSQL extension), or custom (self-managed).

fields.label: FTS Backend
fields.description: Backend for full-text search: sqlite_fts (embedded SQLite FTS5) or custom (external search engine).

fields.label: Default Retrieval Mode
fields.description: How memories are provided to agents: inject (automatically include in prompt), tool_only (agent requests via tool), or hybrid (both).

fields.label: Max Items to Inject
fields.description: Maximum number of memories to include in a single prompt. Prevents context overflow.

fields.label: Max Tokens to Inject
fields.description: Maximum tokens to use for injected memories. Acts as a budget to prevent exceeding context window limits.
